findings.detail: Wave 2 header (line 282) reads 'Tasks 3, 6, 7 in parallel — disjoint file sets.' The actual tasks inside Wave 2 are Task 3, Task 4, and Task 5. Task 6 is in Wave 3; Task 7 is in the Audit Wave. The stale numbers are a residue of the round-1 task compaction that renumbered Tasks 6→4, 7→5, and moved the old parallel-task identifiers but not the header. An executor following this spec would attempt to run Tasks 6 and 7 in Wave 2 — tasks that either don't belong there or don't yet exist — and cannot determine the correct parallelisation boundary. The preamble (line 260) correctly describes Tasks 3 and 5 as serialised across waves, which contradicts the Wave 2 header listing Task 5 (≡ old Task 7) as a Wave-2 parallel member. The authoritative parallelisation directive must be correct.

Implementation Tasks preamble (line 260) states 'ChatViewModel.kt is touched by Tasks 3 and 5 — they sit in different waves to serialise. ChatScreen.kt is touched only by Task 5.' This is doubly wrong after restructure: (1) Task 6 (Wave 3) also modifies ChatViewModel.kt and ChatScreen.kt — the serialisation chain is Task 3 → Task 6, which is correct in the wave structure but invisible from the preamble. (2) ChatScreen.kt is modified by Task 6, not Task 5 (Task 5 is patterns.md only). A reader of the preamble cannot reconstruct the actual file-ownership chain and may schedule Task 5 and Task 6 in parallel thinking they own different files. The preamble is decision-level content that must be accurate.

findings.requirement: US-8 / AC-2.2: Settings changes made before first message apply to that first message
findings.detail: Round 1 added a Deviation entry clarifying that AC-2.2 is satisfied by existing DataStore → bootstrap re-merge → first inference read plumbing. The chain is now documented but no task owns AC-2.2 verification. The clarification entry notes 'No additional plumbing needed' — which is correct — but no task's description or Verify-user field includes a step to confirm this path works end-to-end. Task 10 (Pre-deploy QA) lists AC-2.2 as a Verify-user item on Honor 200, which is the closest coverage. The gap is that there is no unit-test covering the draft-state settings persistence path, and the Testing Strategy section does not mention this omission. For a size-S bugfix relying on pre-existing code, the absence of a regression guard for AC-2.2's data-flow is a minor (not critical) gap.

findings.requirement: US-10 / AC-3.1, US-11 / AC-3.2 — Task 6 stale comment note
findings.detail: Task 6 description (line 310) states 'Also remove the now-stale comment in ChatScreen.kt (the line about MainActivity setting decorFitsSystemWindows — becomes implicit after Task 6).' The comment refers to MainActivity calling enableEdgeToEdge(), which is implemented in Task 4, not Task 6. The parenthetical 'after Task 6' should read 'after Task 4'. This is a minor wording error but it misstates which task makes the comment stale, which could confuse a reviewer checking whether Task 6 is correctly scoped.

summary: 11/12 requirements covered, 1 partial (AC-2.2 — existing-plumbing path documented in deviations but no unit regression guard). 0 missing. The round-1 wave restructure introduced two critical structural gaps in the task orchestration metadata: Wave 2 header still names old task numbers (3, 6, 7) instead of the post-compaction set (3, 4, 5), and the Implementation Tasks preamble omits Task 6's ownership of ChatViewModel.kt and ChatScreen.kt while incorrectly attributing ChatScreen.kt to Task 5. Both are critical because an executor using these as scheduling directives will parallelise incorrectly and produce file-edit conflicts. Two minor findings: AC-2.2 has no unit regression guard, and Task 6 misstates which task makes the stale comment obsolete. Status: fail (2 critical findings).

round1_fixes_verified.ac22_traceability: RESOLVED — Deviation entry added, chain documented. Partial coverage downgraded from minor-gap to minor-partial (no regression test).
round1_fixes_verified.deviation_marker_inconsistency: RESOLVED — All 5 entries now use [APPROVED IN PRE-DRAFT] format. Last entry uses the qualified form '[APPROVED IN PRE-DRAFT — implicit via AC-1.4/1.5 logging requirements]', consistent with R1 minor finding, no escalation.
round1_fixes_verified.task_numbering_compaction: PARTIAL — Task numbering itself is correct (Tasks 1–10 sequential). However, the compaction introduced stale references in Wave 2 header and Implementation Tasks preamble that were not updated. These are the two new critical findings.
